response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with a broad portfolio, but fails to meet the most critical minimum qualification: 8 years of C++ experience. C++ appears only in a 6-month role at National Instruments (2007–2008) and a bachelor thesis project, totaling roughly 6–12 months of practical use. The primary language stack throughout the career is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Additionally, there is no evidence of technical leadership over cloud or DevOps engineering teams, and no production cloud compute platform ownership — the cloud exposure is limited to using AWS CDK as a consumer, not building or leading a compute platform.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering experience with demonstrated comfort across distributed systems and high-throughput services (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP req/s)
Strong culture of engineering craft — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and mentoring/teaching documented across multiple roles and workshops
Staff-level trajectory with cross-team technical influence at re:cinq, including AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling work
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2007–2008 plus a thesis); the role requires 8 years minimum — this is a hard disqualifier
No evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform; AWS CDK usage in a product team is not equivalent to building large-scale compute/storage/network infrastructure
No documented experience managing technical strategy, project priorities, or leading a team of cloud/DevOps engineers, which are core leadership responsibilities for this role
